title: Odyssey, Book 6: Nausicaa
introduction: Book 6 of the Odyssey: Nausicaa. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.original_text: Ὣς ὁ μὲν ἔνθα καθεῦδε πολύτλας δῖος Ὀδυσσεύς.
translation.text: Much-enduring Odysseus slept on the shore. Athena sent a dream to Nausicaa, daughter of King Alcinous. She went to the river to wash clothes with her handmaidens. They played ball, and their shouts woke Odysseus. He emerged from the bushes, naked, covering himself with a branch. The girls fled, but Nausicaa stood her ground. She gave him clothes and directed him to the palace.
